mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of constrained capacity utilization signals supply bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, causing negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during supply-constrained periods, with t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly drop in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized reflects severe demand contraction, immediately depressing equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with a flat federal funds rate indicates unanchored inflation expectations, forcing delayed monetary tightening that suppresses quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL and stable FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth exceeding 500,000 per month during late-cycle expansions signals overheating labor markets that precede central bank tightening and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: High PAYEMS growth during late-cycle phases correlates with positive 20-day forward returns, invalidating the overheating transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals abrupt supply-side shocks that trigger immediate negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where INDPRO drops below the 0.8% threshold.
